projectDesc.p: The project DHARMA has received funding from the European Research Council (ERC) under the European Union's Horizon 2020 research and innovation programme (grant agreement no 809994).

prefixDef.p: Public URIs with the prefix bib to point to a Zotero Group Library named ERC-DHARMA whose data are open to the public.
Internal URIs using the part prefix to point to person elements in the DHARMA_IdListMembers_v01.xml file.

revisionDesc.change: Updating toward the encoding template v03

div.p:
gurudevatДЃdhidaivataviЕ›eб№ЈaбёҐ Е›rД«mДЃб№Ѓ Е›rД«matyДЃб№Ѓ devyДЃб№Ѓ droб№‡abha
б№­б№­ДЃrikДЃyДЃm utpannaбёҐ Е›rД«mahДЃrДЃjanДЃgabalaбёҐ

lg.l:
taie
nДЃa hy asau nб№›patinДЃgabaleti nДЃmnДЃ

div.p:
tatas tasya puttras ta
tpДЃdДЃnudhyДЃtaбёҐ paramamДЃheЕ›varaбёҐ paramabrahmaб№‡yaбёҐ pa
ramagurudevatДЃdhidaivataviЕ›eб№ЈaбёҐ Е›rД«mДЃб№Ѓ Е›rД«matyДЃб№Ѓ devyДЃб№Ѓ
Indrabhaб№­б№­ДЃrikДЃyДЃm utpannaбёҐ Е›rД«mahДЃrДЃjabharataбёҐ

app.note: The character for Е›rД«В° seems to be corrupted.

div.p: His VatsarДЃja's son - who was favoured by him his respected father, who was entirely devoted to Ељiva, who was the foremost patron of brahmins, the distinguished one who was entirely devoted to his teacher/elders and deities вЂ“ was the illustrious MahДЃrДЃja NДЃgabala, born from the illustrious queen Droб№‡abhaб№­б№­ДЃrikДЃ

He whose army вЂ“ surrounded by the glittering of flashing weapons, with elephants that have their temples moist with the flow of rut-fluid and the space in between their temples filled with fluttering banners вЂ“ shines on the van of the battlefield.

The dust scattered over a long way by the falling hoofs of his horses, along the road that his elephants are said to have followed, that dust is settled by the water sprouting from their, i.e. the elephants' large trunks. Therefore he is known by the name of king NДЃgabala Elephant Force.

After him, his NДЃgabalaвЂ™s son вЂ“ who was favoured by him his respected father, who was entirely devoted to Ељiva, who was the foremost patron of brahmins, the distinguished one who was entirely devoted to his teacher/elders and deities вЂ“ was the illustrious MahДЃrДЃja Bharata, born from the illustrious queen Indrabhaб№­б№­ДЃrikДЃ.

Like KДЃrttikeya to ЕљailendraputrД«, to her Indrabhaб№­б№­ДЃrikДЃ вЂ“ who possessed the virtues of compassion and integrity, and who was fully endowed with generosity and amiability вЂ“ was born a son Indra, whose beauty was spotless and lovely.

It is clear from the contents of the text that this plate was meant to be the second plate in a set. The fact that the backside of the plate is not used is peculiar and raises the suspicion that either the charter has never been finished, or the engraver deviated from the standard and used more plates.

Sircar 347-348265 has argued that the epithet paramagurudevatДЃdhidaivataviЕ›eб№ЈaбёҐ in the introduction should be emended to paramagurudaivatДЃdhidaivataviЕ›eб№ЈaбёҐ, as an elaboration of paramadaivata. He translates the compound with one who is a great teacher (of his subjects) and devotee of the gods and the supreme god. It seems unlikely that the word adhidaivata refers to the kingвЂ™s devotion to the supreme god, however, because that devotion is already expressed in the epithet paramamДЃheЕ›vara. The interpretation of the compound as translated here is provided by Yuko Yokochi, cf. 274
The translation of incomplete passages is based on parallel passages in the BamhanД« and MalhДЃr plates of the same king.
First mentioned by Bajpai ; edited by Bosma 273-277 from a photograph of the original; re-edited here by Natasja Bosma.
